      "それは、地域レベルでスポーツを組織し促進するクラブを持っています。"
    ],

      "それは、ハンドラーと犬の間の絆を特徴としています。",
      "それは、距離キャッチのようなイベントを特徴としています。",
      "それは、フライングディスクの使用を特徴としています。",
      "それは、1974年8月5日の決定的な瞬間を特徴としています。",
      "それは、全国放送された野球の試合を特徴としています。",
      "それは、アシュリー・ウィペットという名前の犬を特徴としています。",
      "それは、犬が高速でジャンプしてディスクをキャッチする能力を特徴としています。",
      "それは、フリースタイルイベントでの音楽の使用を特徴としています。",
      "それは、フリースタイルイベントでの主観的な判定を特徴としています。"
    ],

      "それは、オックスフォードシャー州バンベリーのドッグトレーニングバーンで開催されました。",
      "それは、いくつかの国で開催されました。"

    "KnownAs": [

    "KnownFor": [
      "それは距離キャッチで知られています。",
      "それはフリースタイルキャッチで知られています。",

    "OccurredDuring": [

      "それは、1週間にわたるワールドチャンピオンシップの壮大なイベント中に発生しました。"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それは1970年代初頭に発生しました。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国で発生しました。",
      "それはヨーロッパで発生しました。",

      "それはフリースタイルキャッチに使用されます。",

      "それはトス＆フェッチイベントで使用されます。",

About: It is about a dog sport.
Affects: It affects the bond between handler and dog.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the rise in popularity of Frisbee sport.
ConsistsOf: It consists of dogs and their human flying disc throwers competing in events.
It consists of short routines choreographed to music with multiple discs in play.
CreatedAround: It was created around the early 1970s.
HasA: It has teams of one person and one dog competing in the standard distance toss and fetch event.
It has competitions that often feature the dynamic freestyle event.
It has divisions usually based on the skill and experience of the handler.
It has contestants who have 60 seconds to make as many throws as possible.
It has dogs awarded points for catches based on the distance of the throw.
It has mid-air catches rating extra points.
It has teams consisting of one person and their dog.
It has routines judged in categories that include canine athleticism, degree of difficulty, and showmanship.
It has organizations running tournaments.
It has clubs organizing and promoting the sport on a local level.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by choreographed freestyle catching.
It is characterized by the bond between handler and dog.
It is characterized by events such as distance catching.
It is characterized by the use of flying discs.
It is characterized by a definitive moment on August 5, 1974.
It is characterized by a nationally broadcast baseball game.
It is characterized by a dog named Ashley Whippet.
It is characterized by a dog's ability to catch discs at high speeds and leaps.
It is characterized by the use of music in freestyle events.
It is characterized by subjective judging in freestyle events.
HeldIn: It was held in The Dog Training Barn, Banbury, Oxfordshire.
It was held in various countries.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the rise in popularity of Frisbee sport.
IsA: It is a popular attraction at sporting events.
It is a popular attraction in amusement parks, county fairs, and pet festivals.
KnownAs: It is known as Frisbee dog.
KnownFor: It is known for distance catching.
It is known for freestyle catching.
It is known for the bond between handler and dog.
It is known for canine athleticism.
It is known for the degree of difficulty in routines.
It is known for incredible flips, hyperfast multiple catches, and spectacular vaults.
It is known for its accessibility.
It is known for being half-time entertainment at sporting events.
OccurredAt: It occurred at a nationally broadcast baseball game.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during halftime shows for football games.
It occurred during a week-long World Championship extravaganza.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
It occurred in Japan, the Netherlands, Italy, Germany, Poland, Czech Republic, Hungary, Canada, Australia and Turkey.
It occurred in the United Kingdom.
PerformedBy: It was performed by disc dogs.
It was performed by Ashley and his 8th inning performance in 1974.
UsedAs: It is used as half-time entertainment.
It is used as a way to learn more about the sport.
UsedFor: It is used for distance catching.
It is used for freestyle catching.
It is used for raising money and awareness for local animal charities.
It is used for titling in conjunction with its competition series.
UsedIn: It is used in disc dog competitions.
It is used in toss and fetch events.
It is used in freestyle events.
It is used in long-distance events.
It is used in Quadruped Series competitions.
It is used in Skyhoundz competitions.
It is used in UFO World Cup Series.
It is used in USDDN Finals.
It is used in US Disc Dog International Finals.
It is used in Purina Incredible Dog Challenge.
UsedTo: It is used to celebrate the bond between handler and dog.
It is used to work together.
It is used to fuel interest in the sport.
It is used to compete in events.
It is used to make as many throws as possible.
It is used to win the longest catch.
It is used to judge teams in categories.
It is used to spread popularity to Europe.
It is used to offer newcomers a way to learn more about the sport.
It is used to give back to the sport.
